observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and Management agreed to furnish COA with copy of any
relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing
account balances and exert effort in the collection of the cited receivables.

3.6 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Engineer, the Municipal Accountant to locate records of road networks in
their offices where the necessary data, specifications and locations of the
Road Networks may be sourced to facilitate the preparation and
submission of the aforementioned required reports and the proper
recognition of Road Networks and their corresponding depreciation in the
accounting books.

3.7 We also recommended and Management agreed to ensure preparation and
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and its components and
the disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2015-008.

4.9 We recommended and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer agreed to strictly
comply with the requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation
Requests for all expenditures. Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and
Obligations shall be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the
agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment,
Appropriations, Obligations and Balances and the SCBAA.

4.10 We also recommended and Municipal Accountant agreed not to process
claims without the required certification as to the availability of appropri- ation
including the specific cost centers or function/program/project where
expenses should be charged.

7.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the cited ORs and DVs be
submitted immediately to this office. Henceforth, FSs, DVs and ORs should
be submitted within the prescribed period to enable timely audit of account
balances and transact.

8.5 We recommended and Management agreed to immediately require the
concerned personnel to cooperate in the preparation and submission of
AAPSI in order for the audit team to monitor the status of implementation of
audit recommendations and validate the actions taken, if any.

9.8 We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the transparency
provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited in COA
Circular No. 2013-004.

11.6 We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the audit team the
list of beneficiaries of the fertilizers that were supposedly procured and
distributed specifying therein their names, addresses, quantity received and
their signatures for further verification

12.10 We recommended and Management agreed to:
• Formulate hiring plan of casuals/job orders to strengthen internal
controls pertaining thereto. Department heads should be able to provide
reasonable explanation on the need to hire such number of employees
under contracts/job orders; and
• Submit Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution, Contracts of Job Orders,
Accomplishment Reports and certificate or evidence of service rendered
or approved DTR for the abovementioned transactions to avoid audit
suspensions.

13.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2023 to COA for audit
purposes, if any; and
• Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis guide
on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the Revised IRR
of RA No. 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA for audit purposes.

14.5 We recommend that Management submit the approved list and copies of CY
2023 awarded contracts together with duly accomplished supporting
documents as well as purchase orders immediately for auditorial review; and

14.6 We recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Auditor a copy of
the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working
days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together
with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and
evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

15.7 We recommended and Management agreed to submit immediately the
approved list of programs and projects to be implemented and fund
utilization report by the agency from its share from Tobacco Excise Tax and
Burley and Native Tobacco Excise Tax received in CY 2022 and 2023 together
with other relevant documents for the audit team’s evaluation.

16.5 We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Quarterly Reports
on the Utilization of 20% DF using the Full Disclosure Policy (FDP) Form 7 for
audit team’s evaluation.

17.4 We recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the LDRRMF Utilization Report and ensure that implementation
should be based on the planned program/projects identified during the
budget process; and
• Make a report on the fund status and utilization of the LDRRMF and post
it in conspicuous places of the municipality. This is to promote
transparency and to prevent suspicions of any irregularity.

18.6 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the MPDC to discuss
with the LDC the preparation, adoption and updating of the CLUP for CY 2023
onwards.

19.5 We recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit the GAD
Accomplishment Report with complete supporting documents and in the
prescribed format for audit and evaluation.

20.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal Point
System to:
• Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender
analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation:
• Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues
and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the
said issues; and
• Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender
issues.

22.11 We recommended and Management agreed to submit Sangguniang Bayan
Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC, CLJIP as well as the
fund Utilization Report for CY 2023. Henceforth, observe and abide with the
rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization
of the 1% LCPC fund.

We recommended that Management:
• Cash advances be granted only for payment of salaries and wages, honoraria and for other petty operating expenses. Claims above ₱15,000.00 should be made through checks; and
• Purchases of supplies and materials be made through the prescribed modes under RA 9184 and not through cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

A copy thereof be furnished to COA for audit purposes.
We recommended that Management submit the Quarterly Reports on the Utilization of 20% DF using the Full Disclosure Policy (FDP) Form for audit team’s evaluation.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit the required documents mentioned under item 13.3.5 above. Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to furnish the COA with a
copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the
foregoing account balances and exert effort in the collection of the cited
receivables.

4.5 We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant Designate agreed to
provide complete Reconciliation of Surplus/(Deficit) with the Net Cash Flow
from Operating Activities for all funds. Henceforth, strictly comply with the
provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) for
financial reporting purposes.

5.10 We have recommended and the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer agreed to
strictly comply with the requirements of properly accomplishing the
Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Registry of Appropriations,
Allotment and Obligations shall be maintained to properly monitor the
budget of the agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of
Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and Balances and the SCBAA.

5.11 We have also recommended and management agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant not to process claims without the required
certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific cost
centers or function/program/project where expenses should be charged.

6.5 We have recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant with the cooperation of the Municipal Budget Officer provide
reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis to the budget
and actual amounts presented in the statement financial performance.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of International Public
Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting purposes.

8.8 We have recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the
transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations
cited above.

10.10 We have recommended and Management agreed to:
• Formulate hiring plan of casuals/job orders to strengthen internal
controls pertaining thereto. Department heads should be able to provide
reasonable explanation on the need to hire such number of employees
under contracts/job orders; and
• Submit Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution, Contracts of Job Orders,
Accomplishment Reports and certificate or evidence of service rendered
or approved DTR for the abovementioned transactions to avoid audit
suspensions.

11.5 We have recommended and Management agreed that:
• Cash advances should be made only for payment of salaries and wages,
honoraria and for other petty operating expenses. Claims above
₱15,000.00 should be made through checks; and
• Purchases of supplies and materials be made through the prescribed
modes under RA 9184 and not through cash advances.

12.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2021 to COA for audit
purposes, if any; and
• Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis
guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA for audit
purposes.

13.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to submit the approved list
and copies of CY 2021 awarded contracts together with duly accomplished
supporting documents as well as purchase orders immediately for auditorial
review; and

13.6 Henceforth, furnish the Auditor a copy of the purchase orders and contract
documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase
orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished
supporting documents for auditorial and technical review and evaluation in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

14.7 We have recommended and Management agreed to submit immediately the
approved list of programs and projects to be implemented and fund
utilization report by the agency from its share from Tobacco Excise Tax and
Burley and Native Tobacco Excise Tax received in CY 2020 and 2021
together with the other relevant documents for the audit team’s evaluation.

15.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to submit immediately the
approved 20% Development Fund accomplishment report for the audit
team’s evaluation.

16.4 We have recommended and Management agreed to:
• Submit the LDRRMF Utilization Report and ensure that implementation
should be based on the planned program/projects identified during the
budget process;
• Make a report on the fund status and utilization of the LDRRMF and
post it in conspicuous places of the municipality. This is to promote
transparency and to prevent suspicions of any irregularity; and
• Ensure that all related disclosures on LDRRMF are reflected in the Notes
to Financial Statements.

17.6 We have recommended and management agreed to instruct the MPDC to
discuss with the LDC the preparation, adoption and updating of the CLUP
and CDP for CY 2022 onwards.

18.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit the
GAD Accomplishment Report with complete supporting documents and in
the prescribed format for audit and evaluation.

19.5 We have recommended and management agreed require the GAD Focal
Point System to:
• Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender
analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation:
• Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues
and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to
the said issues; and
• Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for
particular gender issues.

policies.

We have recommended that management:
 Submit Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution, Contracts of Job Orders, Accomplishment Reports and certificate or evidence of service rendered or approved DTR for the abovementioned transactions to avoid audit suspensions.
 Personnel who have been working with the Municipality for more than 6 months may be hired as a casual employee charged under PS budget especially those who are performing essential and necessary services of a regular staff considering the ability of the Municipality to appropriate items under PS budget without exceeding the prescribed PS limitation.

We have recommended the following:
 That the lacking documents enumerated in item 11.3 and 11.5 be immediately submitted to COA for evaluation and review.
observations.reason: Lacking documents not yet submitted

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit a copy of an approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2018. Henceforth, ensure that an APP be prepared and approved before any procurement will be made.

In view of the foregoing observations, Required documents and contracts not yet submitted we have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the CY 2019 noted lapses and submit the required documents mentioned under item 13.3.5 above. Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA 9184.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the lacking documents required. Succeeding transactions shall be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims and processing of payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be discontinued

1.7 We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to furnish the
COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information
about the foregoing receivable balances and exert effort in the collection of
said receivables.

3.6 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to locate records of road networks
in their offices where the necessary data, specifications and locations of the
Road Networks may be sourced to facilitate the preparation and
submission of the aforementioned required reports and the proper
recognition of Road Networks and their corresponding depreciation in the
accounting books.

3.7 We also recommended and Management agreed to ensure preparation and
maintenance of subsidiary ledgers for the roads and its components and the
disclosure thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

4.5 We recommended and Management agreed to secure relevant records
containing adequate information and prepare the required subsidiary
ledger to validly support the above-mentioned account balances.

5.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to provide
complete and correct Reconciliation of Surplus/(Deficit) with the Net Cash
Flow from Operating Activities for all funds. Henceforth, strictly comply
with the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standards
(IPSAS) for financial reporting purposes.

6.8 We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to strictly
comply with the requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation
Requests for all expenditures. Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and
Obligations shall be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the
agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment,
Appropriations, Obligations and Balances and the SCBAA.

6.9 We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant not to process claims without the required certification as to
the availability of appropriation including the specific cost centers or
function/program/project where expenses should be charged.

7.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
with the cooperation of the Municipal Budget Officer provide
reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis to the budget
and actual amounts presented in the statement financial performance.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of International Public
Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) for financial reporting purposes.

8.9 We recommended that management:
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit the CY
2019 accounts and documents cited;
 Justify the noted lapses; and
 Henceforth, submit all the financial reports within prescribed period.

9.5 We recommended and management agreed to submit the approved list and
copies of CY 2019 awarded contracts together with duly accomplished
supporting documents as well as purchase orders immediately for
auditorial review; and

9.6 Henceforth, furnish the Auditor a copy of the purchase orders and contract
documents not later than five working days from issuance of purchase
orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished
supporting documents for auditorial and technical review and evaluation in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

10.7 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management
agreed:
 That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers
are properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall
a disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting documents;
and
 That the lacking documents enumerated above be immediately
submitted to COA for evaluation and review.

11.10 In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended and
Management agreed to:
 Formulate hiring plan of casuals/ contractuals/ job orders to strengthen
internal controls pertaining thereto. Department heads should be able
to provide reasonable explanation on the need to hire such number of
employees under contracts/job orders; and
 Submit Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution, Contracts of Job Orders,
Accomplishment Reports and certificate or evidence of service rendered
or approved DTR for the abovementioned transactions to avoid audit
suspensions.

12.6 We recommended and Management agreed with the following:
 That Management grants cash advances only for payment of salaries
and wages, honoraria and for other petty operating expenses. Claims
above ₱15,000.00 should be made through checks;
 Purchases of supplies and materials be made through the prescribed
modes under RA 9184 and not through cash advances.

13.6 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management
agreed:
 That the Municipal Accountant sees to it that all disbursement vouchers
are properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall a
disbursement be paid without the necessary supporting documents;
and
 That the lacking documents enumerated in item 2.1 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 be immediately submitted to COA for evaluation and
review.

14.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the
lacking documents and ensure that in future transactions all required
documents are attached and reviewed before processing the disbursement
vouchers and making payment thereof. Ensure that rules and regulations
with regard to the procurement of medicines and other health related
supplies are observed at all times.

15.5 We recommended that Management:
 Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2019 to COA for audit
purposes, if any; and
 Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis
guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7.2 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA for audit
purposes.

15.6 Management agreed with the audit recommendations and will request training
for BAC members.

16.5 We recommended and management agreed to justify the noted lapses and
submit immediately the approved list of programs and projects to be
implemented and accomplishment report by the agency from its share from
Tobacco Excise Tax and Burley and Native Tobacco Excise Tax received in
CY 2019 together with the other relevant documents, for the audit team’s
evaluation.

17.4 We recommended and management agreed to justify the noted lapses and
submit immediately the approved 20% Development Fund accomplishment
report for the audit team’s evaluation.

18.5 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and management
agreed to:
 Submit the LDRRMF Utilization Report and ensure that
implementation should be based on the planned program/projects
identified during the budget process;
 Make a report on the fund status and utilization of the LDRRMF and
post it in conspicuous places of the municipality. This is to promote
transparency and to prevent suspicions of any irregularity;
 Ensure that all related disclosures on LDRRMF are reflected in the
Notes to Financial Statements;
 Appropriate funds for the procurement of rescue equipment for these
will last longer and thus provide more benefit to the constituents.

19.4 We recommended and management agreed to justify the noted lapses and
submit immediately the approved Special Education Fund (SEF)
accomplishment report together with the disbursement vouchers, for the
audit team’s evaluation.

20.6 We recommended that management justify the noted lapses and instruct
the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation, adoption and updating
of the CLUP and CDP for CY 2020 onwards.

20.7 Management agreed with the audit recommendations. As to date, the CLUP is
still on-going

21.11 We recommended and Management agreed to justify the noted lapses.
Submit the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution or Administrative Order
creating the LCPC, CLJIP as well as the fund Utilization Report for CY
2019. Henceforth, observe and abide with the rules and regulations with
regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

22.5 We recommended and Management agreed to immediately submit the
GAD Accomplishment Report with complete supporting documents and in
the prescribed format for audit and evaluation.

Lacking documents cited above should be submitted for audit.
 Municipal Accountant to:
- maintain subsidiary ledger for individual loan releases and reconcile with the amount appearing in the financial statements.
- take extra care in classifying account balances by adhering to prescribed accounting rules to present fairly the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
1.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
1.3.1 Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to make
representations with the Land Bank of the Philippines for the
reconciliation of their individual records and obtain copies of debit memos
and other related loan documents as reference in adjusting the books to
reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.
Lacking documents cited above should be submitted for audit.
1.3.2 Municipal Accountant to:
1.3.2.1 maintain subsidiary ledger for individual loan releases and
reconcile with the amount appearing in the financial statements.
1.3.2.2 take extra care in classifying account balances by adhering to
prescribed accounting rules to present fairly the financial
statements.

2.8 We have recommended that the:
2.8.1 Municipal Budget Officer strictly comply with the requirements of properly
accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Registry of
Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations. These reports shall be
maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the
basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations
and Balances and the SCBAA.
2.8.2 Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims
without the required certification as to the availability of appropriation
including the specific cost centers or function/program/project where
expenses should be charged.

3.5 We have recommended that management implement the following measures to
assure the reliability of balances of PPE accounts:


- Physical inventory of fixed assets shall be made at least once a year;
- Establishment of property identification and coding;
- Maintenance of the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment to establish the
official accountable for the property;
- Reconciliation of the inventory report with the balances reflected in the
financial statements, identification of persons accountable for loss of property,
and proper adjustment in the financial statements, if any; and
- Maintenance of updated Property Card and Property Plant and Equipment
Ledger Cards.

4.7 We have recommended that management furnish the COA with a copy of any
relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing receivable
balances and exert effort in the collection of said receivables.

5.6 We have recommended that Management:
5.6.1 direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to locate
records of road networks in their offices where the necessary data,
specifications and locations of the Road Networks may be sourced to
facilitate the recognition of road networks and the corresponding
depreciation in the accounting books, the preparation of subsidiary
ledgers and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.
5.6.2 ensure compliance with the conduct of inventory and the recognition of
Road Networks in the books of accounts as well as the disclosure thereof
in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-
008.

6.4. We have recommended that management coordinate with the DepED Division
of Abra for the proper turnover of the School Buildings still being maintained in
the books of the municipality. Based on the turnover documents, the municipal
accountant shall derecognize the school buildings from its books of accounts.

7.5 We have recommended that management secure relevant records containing
adequate information and prepare the required subsidiary ledger to validly
support the above-mentioned account balances.

8.9 We have recommended that:
8.9.1 the FSs, DVs and ORs be submitted immediately to this office by the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer. Henceforth, these reports be submitted
within the prescribed period to enable timely audit of account balances and
transactions.
8.9.10 the Municipal Mayor hire a full time Municipal Accountant or assign additional
personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office to augment personnel in the
preparation of the required reports and documents and facilitate timely
submission of accounts.

9.6 We have recommended the following:
9.6.1 That Management grant cash advances only for payment of salaries and
wages, honoraria and for other petty operating expenses. Claims above
₱15,000.00 should be made through checks;
9.6.2 Purchases of supplies and materials be made through the prescribed
modes under RA 9184 and not through cash advances. Likewise, in no
case shall payment of implemented projects be made out of cash advances
as the same should be made directly to the suppliers or contractors;
9.6.3 Require the officials/employees to liquidate their cash advances. In all
instances, no additional cash advances shall be granted unless previous
ones are liquidated or duly accounted for; and
9.6.4 The Municipal Mayor looks into the possibility of withholding the
salaries and claims of officials and employees who fail to account or
liquidate their cash advance.

10.12 We have recommended that management:
10.12.1 Formulate hiring plan of casuals/ contractuals/ job orders to
strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto. Department heads
should be able to provide reasonable explanation on the need to hire
such number of employees under contracts/job orders.
10.12.2 Submit Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution, Contracts of Job
Orders, Accomplishment Reports and certificate or evidence of
service rendered or approved DTR for the abovementioned
transactions to avoid audit suspensions.
10.12.3 Personnel who have been working with the Municipality for more
than 6 months may be hired as a casual employee charged under PS
budget especially those who are performing essential and necessary
services of a regular staff considering the ability of the Municipality
to appropriate items under PS budget without exceeding the
prescribed PS limitation.

12.5 We have recommended that management submit a copy of an approved
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for CY 2018. Henceforth, ensure that an
APP be prepared and approved before any procurement will be made.

13.8 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the Bids and
Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the required
documents mentioned under item 13.3.5 above. Henceforth, comply with the
rules and regulations as required by RA 9184.

• That the Municipal Budget Officer be instructed to strictly comply with the requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations shall be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and Balances; and
• The Municipal Accountant is instructed not to process claims without the required certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses should be charged.

• Accomplishment reports should be evaluated vis-à-vis the terms/services agreed upon in the contract by personnel of the HRMO.

Municipal Accountant – Designate and should reconcile with the amount
appearing in the financial statements.
1.5 We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate take extra
care in classifying account balances by adhering to prescribed accounting
regulations to present fairly the financial statements.

2.4 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the
bases in the preparation of the financial statements.

3.8 We have recommended that management implement the following measures to
assure the reliability of balances of PPE accounts:
 Physical inventory of fixed assets shall be made at least once a year;
 Establishment of property identification and coding;
 Maintenance of the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment to establish the
official accountable for the property;
 Reconciliation of the inventory report with the balances reflected in the
financial statements, identification of persons accountable for loss of property,
and proper adjustment in the financial statements, if any; and
 Maintenance of updated Property Card and Property Plant and Equipment
Ledger Cards.

4.8 We have recommended that management furnish the COA with a copy of any
relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing receivable
balances and exert effort in the collection of said receivables. In the absence of
such documents, a request for authority to write-off together with a valid
justification should be filed in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

5.8 We have recommended that:
 Management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system and the
GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
 The Municipal Accountant trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which
were closed to government equity in the prior years and subsequently record in
its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required
transition provision.

6.5 We have recommended that the disbursement vouchers should be submitted
together with the disbursement journals every 10th day of the following month.
6.6 We have also recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire a full time Municipal
Accountant or assign additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office
to augment personnel in the preparation of needed documents and reports and to
facilitate early submission of accounts.

7.2 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
 That Management grants cash advances only for payment of salaries and wages,
honoraria and for other petty operating expenses. Claims above ₱15,000.00
should be made through checks;
 Purchases of supplies and materials be made through the prescribed modes
under RA 9184 and not through cash advances. Likewise, in no case shall
payment of implemented projects be made out of cash advances as the same
should be made directly to the suppliers or contractors;
 In all instances, no additional cash advances shall be granted unless previous
ones are liquidated or duly accounted for; and
 The Municipal Mayor looks into the possibility of withholding the salaries and
claims of officials and employees who fail to account or liquidate their cash
advance.

8.7 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
 That the Municipal Budget Officer be instructed to strictly comply with the
requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all
expenditures. Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations shall be
maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the basis
to prepare the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and
Balances; and
 The Municipal Accountant is instructed not to process claims without the
required certification as to the availability of appropriation including the
specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses should be
charged.

9.7 In view of the foregoing audit observations, we have recommended the following
courses of action:
 The Municipality formulate a hiring plan of casuals/contractuals/job orders to
strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto. Department heads should be able
to provide reasonable explanation on the need to hire such number of employees
under contracts/job orders.
 The functions and duties of the contracted employees must be well defined in
their contracts and are clear, known and accepted by employees prior to
rendition of services to facilitate proper evaluation of work performed by these
employees.
Accomplishment reports should be evaluated vis-à-vis the terms/services agreed
upon in the contract by personnel of the HRMO.
 Employees who have been working with the Municipality for more than 6 months
may be hired as a casual employee charged under PS budget especially those who
are performing essential and necessary services of a regular stall considering the
ability of the Municipality to appropriate items under PS budget without
exceeding the prescribed PS limitation.

10.6 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
 That the Municipal Accountant see to it that all disbursement vouchers are
properly supported with complete documentation. In no case shall a
disbursement be made without the necessary supporting documents; and
 That the lacking documents be immediately submitted to COA for evaluation and
review.

11.6 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the
lacking documents required. Succeeding transactions shall be properly
supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of
claims and processing of payment of claims that are not properly documented
shall be discontinued.
11.7 Management should strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA
9184 and GPPB Resolution 0188-2006 whenever it implements infrastructure
projects by administration such as Pakyaw System.

12.9 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the Bids and
Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the required documents
mentioned above. Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required
by RA 9184.

observations.managementAction: Improvements were observed
observations.reason: but a lot still needs to be done.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that a summary of land titles/tax declarations be submitted to support the balances appearing in the financial statements. Management should also submit proof of ownership of the land where the municipal hall and the gymnasium are located.
We have also recommended that management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lot through a Transfer of Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.

We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses, submit the Official Receipts/Sales Invoices for the above mentioned disbursements. Henceforth, the name of the supplier/business establishment should be properly identified in the disbursement voucher.

We have recommended that management furnish the Auditor a copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
We have recommended that an Annual Procurement Plan be prepared to plan and schedule the procurement activities of the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that Bids and Awards Committee justify and noted lapses and submit the required documents mentioned above. Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA 9184.
observations.reason: Improvements were noted but a lot still needs to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that the management:
 Justify the above noted lapses;
 Consider utilizing he fund for rooted developments which are permanent in nature to benefit more the constituents of the Municipality; and
 Adhere strictly with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the IRA for development projects.
observations.reason: Not all recommendations were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding internal revenue taxes as mandated by laws, rules and regulations on all its purchases and contracts for additional income of the government and to avoid possible imposition of sanctions by the BIR.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the Land Bank of the Philippines for the reconciliation of their individual records and obtain copies of debit memos and other related loan documents as reference in adjusting the books to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.  Subsidiary ledger for individual loan release should also be maintained by the Municipal Accountant-Designate and should reconcile with the amount appearing in the general ledgers and financial statements.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the bases in the preparation of financial statements.  The Municipal Accountant – Designate or the Budget Officer should maintain the required registries to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments.  Likewise, the Obligation Requests which is the basis for recording of obligations in the registries should be properly accomplished to facilitate monitoring of expenses and balances of appropriations.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses.  Henceforth, submit the Official Receipt/ Sales Invoice for the above mentioned disbursements.  Name of the supplier/business establishment should be properly identified in the disbursement voucher.

We have recommended that the disbursement vouchers totaling P 9,655,000.00 be submitted to the Commission on Audit the soonest considering that these have been long overdue for submission.  Henceforth, all financial accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit every 10th day of the following month.  Likewise, the Municipal Mayor should look into the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate submission of accounts.

We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses, submit the contract documents for evaluation and review, henceforth, furnish to the auditor copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting documents for auditorial and technical review in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the lacking documents required.  Succeeding transactions shall be properly supported with complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims and processing of payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be discontinued.

We have recommended that management deduct from the contractor’s succeeding progress billings for contracts due, if any, the excess amount of payments made.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that amount of progress billings paid to the contractor equals to the contract amount of the project.

We have recommended that the management justify the above noted observations; henceforth, payments should be made only after the verification of Statement of Work Accomplished as well as issuance of inspection reports by the concerned LGU personnel.

We have recommended that management enforce the collection of liquidated damages from supplier.  Henceforth, the LGU should properly monitor all its projects and in the event that any contractor/ supplier failed to accomplish the project within the expected delivery period/ contract duration, the related liquidated damages and other appropriate actions be enforced immediately.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate deduct 10% retention money from the gross amount for all succeeding payment of progress/final billing.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by COA Circular N0. 2013-004.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses.  Henceforth, SEF  budget duly supported with programs, projects and activities of the school board for the ensuing fiscal year should be prepared.  All planned programs/projects identified during the budget process be implemented within the time frame to meet the goals set and deliver effective services to the public.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses.  The Budget Officer shall incorporate in programs, projects and activities of their Annual Budget a total cost of at least 5% of their total budget appropriations as authorized under the Annual General Appropriations Act to address the identified gender issues and properly accomplish and certify the availability of appropriation in the Obligation Request.

1.3	We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to make representations with the Land Bank of the Philippines for the reconciliation of their individual records and obtain copies of debit memos and other related loan documents as reference in adjusting the books to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.  Lacking documents cited above should be submitted for audit.
1.4 	Subsidiary ledger for individual loan releases should also be maintained by the Municipal Accountant – Designate and should reconcile with the amount appearing in the financial statements.
1.5	We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate take extra care in classifying account balances by adhering to prescribed accounting regulations to present fairly the financial statements.
2.4	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the bases in the preparation of the financial statements.

4.8	We have recommended that management furnish the COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing receivable balances and exert effort in the collection of said receivables.  In the absence of such documents, a request for authority to write-off together with a valid justification should be filed in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2016-005.

5.8	We have recommended that:
Management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system and the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
The Municipal Accountant trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which were closed to government equity in the prior years and subsequently record in its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required transition provision.

9.7	In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
That the Municipal Budget Officer be instructed to strictly comply with the requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation Requests for all expenditures. Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations shall be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and Balances; and
The Municipal Accountant is instructed not to process claims without the required certification as to the availability of appropriation including the specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses should be charged.

10.4	We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the Official Receipts/Sales Invoices for the above mentioned disbursements.  Henceforth, the name of the supplier/business establishment should be properly identified in the disbursement voucher.

11.4	We have recommended that management furnish the Auditor copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five (5) working days from issuance of purchase orders and perfection of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
12.5	We have recommended that an Annual Procurement Plan be prepared to plan and schedule the procurement activities of the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184.
15.4	We have recommended that management justify the above noted lapses. Henceforth, implementation of projects should be within the amount of appropriation.

16.8	In view of the foregoing audit observations, we have recommended that management:
Justify the above noted lapses;
Consider utilizing the fund for rooted developments which are permanent in nature to benefit more the constituents of the Municipality; and
Adhere strictly with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the IRA for development projects.

17.4	In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
The Budget Officer incorporate in the programs, projects and activities of their Annual Budget a total cost of at least 5% of their total budget appropriations as authorized under the Annual General Appropriations Act; and
Ensure that expenditures are in consonance to the amount approved in the budget and see to it that all planned projects and activities are implemented.

18.6	We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding internal revenue taxes as mandated by laws, rules and regulations on all its purchases and contracts for additional income of the government and to avoid possible imposition of sanctions by the BIR.
We have recommended proposed adjusting entries to correct the recorded balances and reflect the correct amount of the accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses.  Henceforth, promptly remit all the compulsory and mandatory obligations to their respective recipient agencies so as not to deprive the municipal officials and employees the benefits expected from their contributions to avoid the imposition of additional fines and penalties and to preclude misuse of the fund for other purposes.

Expenditures should also be limited to the amounts appropriated in the budget to avoid utilizing the amounts appropriated for the remittance of mandatory obligations.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit proof evidencing the deposit of remaining collections on hand and henceforth, all collections should be deposited regularly and intact pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and DBM-DOF Joint Cir. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the required documents mentioned above.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA 9184.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses and submit the required documents mentioned above.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by RA 9184.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses, henceforth, amount of Approved Budget of the Contract (ABC) should tally with the Program of Work (POW).
We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses.  Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by DPWH Department Order No. 22 series of 2015.

In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended the following:

Justify the noted lapses and submit the required documents;
Henceforth, The Municipal Accountant – Designate and Treasurer verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before the processing of claims; and
All disbursement not attached with the required supporting documents should not be processed and should be returned to the respective employee/personnel.
observations.managementAction: Improvements were noted but a lot still needs to be implemented

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the one (1) year defects liability period from the project’s completion date up to the final acceptance of the projects should be fully covered by the Performance Security of the contractor.  Applicable warranty security against structural defects and failures should also be imposed by the contractors.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant – Designate must ensure that the contractors post the warranty security covering the full amount in the first year and renew the same every year thereafter, subject to reduction every year by the amount of depreciation on a straight line basis.

We have also recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct ocular inspection of the infrastructure projects after the one (1) year defects liability period, and in the event that said project manifested structural defects, oblige the contractor to undertake the necessary repair works or forfeit the corresponding warranty security.
observations.managementAction: Not all projects were subjected to warranty security
